title: Background workers OOMKilled, job queue backing up
summary: The async job workers are crash-looping. Queue depth is climbing fast, email/notification jobs are delayed by 40+ minutes, and pods restart every few minutes. No user-facing 5xx yet, but SLAs on async delivery are breaching.
true_root_cause_hidden: Release v3.4.0 of worker-service added an in-process de-duplication cache (a plain dict keyed by job payload) that is never evicted. Under normal volume it grows unbounded until the pod hits its 512Mi memory limit and is OOMKilled, restarting the loop. Node autoscaling lag and a brief Redis latency blip are red herrings.

metrics.name: worker.pod.memory_pct_of_limit
metrics.note: climbs linearly then resets on restart

deploys.change: v3.4.0: add in-process job de-duplication cache (dict keyed by payload hash); minor logging changes
deploys.author: release-bot

logs.msg: Memory cgroup out of memory: Killed process worker (worker-service), exit code 137
starting up, dedupe cache initialized empty
scale-up requested, 2 pods pending, waiting for node
